name: Food & Drink
description: Dishes, ingredients, brands, and culinary culture

questions.q: What is the Italian culinary term for pasta cooked until it is firm to the bite?

source.original_q: If your pasta has just the right bite-firm but not hard-what's the Italian for that?
source.notes: Literally 'to the tooth' in Italian.

questions.q: What compound found in chili peppers is responsible for their spicy heat?

source.original_q: What fiery compound in chili peppers gives them that signature spicy kick?
source.notes: Piperine is in black pepper, allicin in garlic, curcumin in turmeric.

questions.q: By weight, what is the world's most expensive spice?

source.original_q: What's the most expensive spice by weight, often over $10,000 per kilogram?
source.notes: Harvested from Crocus sativus stigmas. ~150,000 flowers per kg of saffron.

questions.q: Which grain is the most widely consumed staple food in the world?

source.original_q: Which grain is the most widely consumed staple food in the world, feeding billions daily?
source.notes: Staple for over 3.5 billion people, primarily in Asia.

questions.q: According to surveys, what is the most popular pizza topping in the United States?

source.original_q: What's the top topping on US pizzas?
source.notes: Appears on ~50-60% of all US pizza orders. YouGov and Harris polls confirm.

questions.q: Which country is the world's largest producer of olive oil?

source.original_q: What's the country that produces the most olive oil?
source.notes: Spain produces ~45% of world olive oil. Italy is second.

questions.q: Which country is the world's largest producer of orange juice?

source.original_q: Which sunny nation takes the top spot as the world's largest orange juice producer?
source.notes: Brazil produces roughly one-third of the world's orange juice.

questions.q: What classic cocktail is made by mixing tequila, lime juice, and triple sec?

source.original_q: What classic cocktail mixes lime juice, tequila, and triple sec into a party favorite?
source.notes: Often served with salt-rimmed glass. Origin debated, mid-20th century Mexico.

questions.q: In the 1930s, Ruth Wakefield accidentally invented which now-iconic cookie at her Toll House Inn?

source.original_q: Which iconic cookie was born in the 1930s at Ruth Wakefield's Toll House Inn, sweetening our lives forever?
source.notes: Wakefield added chopped Nestlé chocolate to her butter cookie recipe around 1938.

questions.q: What is a 'fluffernutter' sandwich made with?

source.original_q: What's the name of the peanut butter and marshmallow creme sandwich?
source.notes: New England staple. Name from Marshmallow Fluff brand. Nearly became MA state sandwich.

questions.q: A 'turducken' is a dish made by stuffing which three birds inside one another?

source.original_q: What's the name of the dish with chicken, duck and turkey?
source.notes: Chicken inside a duck inside a turkey. Popularized by Paul Prudhomme and John Madden.

questions.q: Which fast-food chain, founded by Glen Bell in 1962, specializes in Mexican-inspired food?

source.original_q: Founded by Glen Bell in 1962 which fast food chain brings the party to your table?
source.notes: First location in Downey, California.

questions.q: In what year did McDonald's introduce the Happy Meal?

source.original_q: Which fast food giant introduced the Happy Meal in 1979 to bring smiles and food to kids everywhere?
source.notes: Launched nationwide in the US in June 1979.

questions.q: 'Taste the Rainbow' is the long-running advertising slogan for which candy brand?

source.original_q: Which candy brand tells you to open a bag and go on a flavor adventure with the slogan 'Taste the Rainbow'?
source.notes: Slogan used since 1994.

questions.q: 'Betcha can't eat just one' is the famous slogan of which potato chip brand?

source.original_q: Which potato chip brand says "Betcha can't eat just one"?
source.notes: Slogan introduced in 1963.

questions.q: 'Once you pop, you can't stop' is the catchphrase of which stackable chip brand?

source.original_q: "Once you pop, you can't stop!" What crunchy snack's tagline makes you want more?
source.notes: Later changed to 'Once you pop, the fun don't stop.'

questions.q: What number appears prominently on every bottle and label of Jack Daniel's Tennessee Whiskey?

source.original_q: What number is on every bottle of Jack Daniel's sour mash whiskey?
source.notes: Old No. 7 brand. Origin of the number is debated.

questions.q: Founded in Geneva in 1783, which brand is widely regarded as the world's first commercial soft drink?

source.original_q: The oldest soft drink in the world is which fizzy drink created in 1783?
source.notes: Founded by Johann Jacob Schweppe; first bottled carbonated water. Moved to London 1792.

questions.q: At which famous New York City hotel was the Waldorf Salad invented in the 1890s?

source.original_q: At which hotel was the Waldorf Salad invented?
source.original_a: Waldorf Astoria Hotel in New York City
source.notes: Created by maître d'hôtel Oscar Tschirky around 1896. Apples, celery, mayo.

questions.q: Bouillabaisse, a traditional stew originating from the port city of Marseille, is made primarily with what?

source.original_q: What's in the French stew called Bouillabaisse?
source.notes: Traditionally uses at least three kinds of fresh Mediterranean fish, plus shellfish.

questions.q: Which U.S. city is famous for originating deep-dish pizza?

source.original_q: Which state throws the best pizza parties with deep-dish?
source.notes: Originated at Pizzeria Uno in Chicago, 1943.

questions.q: The bacterium genus Salmonella is named after which American veterinary scientist?

source.original_q: Where does the notorious food-poisoning bacteria 'salmonella' get its splashy name?
source.notes: Daniel Elmer Salmon (1850-1914). The bacterium was actually isolated by his assistant Theobald Smith in 1885.
